questions.question: Which Windows tool is used to view startup programs, running processes, and performance usage?
questions.explanation: Task Manager shows running processes, startup apps, performance graphs, and resource usage.

questions.question: Which Linux command lists files and directories in the current directory?
questions.explanation: The ls command displays the contents of a directory.

questions.question: Which macOS feature is used to create automatic backups to an external drive or network location?
questions.explanation: Time Machine is the built-in macOS backup utility for versioned file and system backups.

questions.question: A technician wants to test basic network connectivity to a server from Windows, Linux, or macOS. Which command is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: The ping command sends ICMP echo requests to test whether a host is reachable.

questions.question: Which file system is the default modern file system used by Windows system volumes?
questions.explanation: NTFS is the standard Windows file system and supports permissions, compression, encryption, and journaling.

questions.question: A computer powers on but reports that no bootable device is found. Which setting should a technician check first in firmware setup?
questions.explanation: The boot order determines which device firmware tries to start from first.

questions.question: Which Windows console is used to view system, security, and application logs?
questions.explanation: Event Viewer displays logs that help diagnose operating system, application, and security events.

questions.question: Which Windows feature downloads and installs operating system patches and driver updates?
questions.explanation: Windows Update provides Microsoft patches, security fixes, feature updates, and some driver updates.

questions.question: A user can read a Windows file but cannot modify or delete it. Which NTFS permission most likely allows this access level?
questions.explanation: Read permission allows opening and viewing a file but does not allow changing or deleting it.

questions.question: Which Windows account type is intended for daily use without full system-wide administrative rights?
questions.explanation: A standard user can run applications and manage personal settings but cannot perform most administrative tasks without elevation.

questions.question: A technician needs to run Linux inside Windows for testing without replacing the host OS. Which solution is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: A virtual machine runs a guest operating system in an isolated environment on a host system.

questions.question: During a clean OS installation, what usually happens to existing applications on the target system partition?
options: They are converted to portable apps
questions.explanation: A clean installation replaces the existing operating system environment, so applications generally need to be reinstalled.

questions.question: A Windows update caused instability, but user files should remain intact. Which recovery option is most appropriate to revert system files and settings to an earlier state?
questions.explanation: System Restore rolls back system files, registry settings, and some configuration changes without targeting personal files.

questions.question: Which Linux command is commonly used on systemd-based distributions to start, stop, or check services?
questions.explanation: systemctl controls and inspects services and other units on systems that use systemd.

questions.question: A Linux application is frozen and its process ID is known. Which command can terminate it?
questions.explanation: The kill command sends a signal to a process, commonly to terminate it by process ID.

questions.question: Which Windows tool is used to initialize disks, create partitions, and assign drive letters?
questions.explanation: Disk Management provides a graphical interface for managing disks, volumes, partitions, and drive letters.

questions.question: A Windows workstation receives an APIPA address beginning with 169.254. What is the most likely issue?
options: The DHCP server was not reached
The monitor refresh rate is incorrect
questions.answer: The DHCP server was not reached
questions.explanation: Windows assigns an APIPA address when it is configured for DHCP but cannot obtain a lease.

questions.question: Which Windows feature encrypts an entire drive to help protect data if a device is lost or stolen?
questions.explanation: BitLocker provides full-volume encryption for supported Windows editions.

questions.question: On iOS and Android, what is the common term for installing OS and app security fixes?
questions.explanation: Mobile devices receive OS and app updates to add features and patch security vulnerabilities.

questions.question: A Windows PC repeatedly crashes after a newly installed driver. Which startup option is useful for loading minimal drivers to troubleshoot?
questions.explanation: Safe Mode starts Windows with a minimal set of drivers and services, making driver problems easier to isolate.

questions.question: Which Windows utility is used to check protected system files and replace corrupted copies?
questions.explanation: System File Checker, run with sfc, scans protected Windows system files and repairs detected corruption.

questions.question: Which directory commonly stores system-wide configuration files on Linux?
questions.explanation: The /etc directory contains many system-wide configuration files.

questions.question: Which macOS security feature encrypts the startup disk?
questions.explanation: FileVault encrypts the Mac startup disk to protect data at rest.

questions.question: A technician needs to display the current working directory in Linux or macOS Terminal. Which command should be used?
questions.explanation: The pwd command prints the full path of the current working directory.

questions.question: Which file system is commonly used by modern macOS startup volumes?
questions.explanation: APFS is Apple's modern file system designed for flash storage and used by current macOS versions.

questions.question: A UEFI system fails to start after its EFI System Partition is deleted. What was most likely stored on that partition?
questions.explanation: UEFI firmware reads bootloaders and related startup files from the EFI System Partition.

questions.question: Which Windows tool lets an administrator create automated jobs that run at specific times or events?
questions.explanation: Task Scheduler runs programs, scripts, and maintenance actions based on schedules or triggers.

questions.question: A Linux administrator on Ubuntu needs to install available package updates. Which command is commonly used after refreshing package lists?
questions.explanation: On Debian-based systems, apt upgrade installs newer versions of installed packages after apt update refreshes package lists.

questions.question: In Linux permissions, what does the execute bit allow on a directory?
questions.explanation: Execute permission on a directory allows a user to access entries within it if they know the names.

questions.question: Which Linux file contains local user account information such as usernames and user IDs?
questions.explanation: The /etc/passwd file stores user account entries, while password hashes are typically stored in /etc/shadow.

questions.question: Which hardware feature is commonly required or recommended for running 64-bit virtual machines efficiently?
questions.explanation: CPU virtualization extensions such as Intel VT-x or AMD-V improve and often enable modern VM support.

questions.question: Which type of installation keeps user files, settings, and compatible applications when moving to a newer OS version?
questions.explanation: An in-place upgrade installs a newer OS while attempting to preserve existing files, settings, and supported apps.

questions.question: A Mac will not boot normally. Which built-in environment can provide Disk Utility, reinstall options, and restore tools?
questions.explanation: macOS Recovery provides tools to repair disks, reinstall macOS, restore from backup, and access startup security options.

questions.question: A Windows service should start automatically whenever the computer boots. Which startup type should be configured?
questions.explanation: The Automatic startup type starts the service during the normal Windows boot process.

questions.question: A Windows computer shows frequent blue screen errors after memory was upgraded. Which initial action is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: New or faulty RAM can cause stop errors, so testing memory is a focused first diagnostic step.

questions.question: Which TWO Linux commands are commonly used to view or manage running processes? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: ps lists processes, and top provides a live view of processes and resource usage.

questions.question: Which TWO Windows tools are commonly used to troubleshoot hardware driver problems? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Device Manager shows device and driver status, while Event Viewer can show related driver or hardware errors.

questions.question: Which TWO macOS tools or features are useful for disk repair and backup recovery? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Disk Utility can check and repair volumes, and Time Machine can restore files or systems from backups.

questions.question: Which TWO Windows commands are used to troubleshoot IP configuration and name resolution? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: ipconfig displays adapter configuration, and nslookup queries DNS name resolution.

questions.question: Which TWO file systems support journaling in typical desktop operating systems? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: NTFS and ext4 are journaling file systems that help recover consistency after crashes.

questions.question: A PC fails immediately after POST with a bootloader error. Which TWO areas should be checked first? (Select all that apply)
options: EFI System Partition or boot files
answer: EFI System Partition or boot files
questions.explanation: Bootloader errors often relate to the selected boot device or missing or damaged boot files.

questions.question: Which TWO Windows tools can show performance or resource utilization information? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Task Manager provides quick resource views, and Performance Monitor collects detailed performance counters.

questions.question: Which TWO actions are best practices before installing a major operating system update? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Backups and compatibility checks reduce the risk of data loss and post-update failures.

questions.question: Which TWO Linux commands are used to change file permissions and ownership? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: chmod changes permission bits, and chown changes file or directory ownership.

questions.question: Which TWO tasks are typically performed when setting up a new local user account? (Select all that apply)
options: Assign the account to appropriate groups
Set an initial password or sign-in method
answer: Assign the account to appropriate groups
Set an initial password or sign-in method
questions.explanation: User setup normally includes authentication and group membership so the account has the correct access.

questions.question: Which TWO resources must be allocated to most virtual machines? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: VMs require RAM and virtual storage, along with CPU access and optional virtual hardware.

questions.question: Which TWO items should be verified before installing a 64-bit operating system? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: A 64-bit OS requires compatible hardware, and driver availability is important for devices to work after installation.

questions.question: Which TWO Windows recovery tools can help when Windows fails to boot correctly? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Startup Repair targets boot problems, while System Restore can reverse problematic system changes.

questions.question: A web service on Linux is not responding. Which TWO commands could help check service status and recent logs on a systemd system? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: systemctl status shows unit state, and journalctl displays systemd journal logs for troubleshooting.

questions.question: Which TWO Windows options can be used to end an unresponsive application? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Task Manager can end processes graphically, and taskkill can terminate processes from the command line.

questions.question: Which TWO tasks are performed with Windows Disk Management? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Disk Management can resize supported volumes and assign or change drive letters.

questions.question: Which TWO settings are normally required for a static IPv4 configuration? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: A static IPv4 configuration requires at least an address and subnet mask, and usually gateway and DNS settings.

questions.question: Which TWO practices improve workstation OS security? (Select all that apply)
options: Use least privilege for daily accounts
Disable the firewall on public networks
answer: Use least privilege for daily accounts
questions.explanation: Timely updates and least-privilege accounts reduce exposure to vulnerabilities and accidental system changes.

questions.question: Which TWO actions help secure a lost mobile device? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Remote lock can prevent access, and remote wipe can remove data if the device cannot be recovered.

questions.question: A Windows application fails only for one user profile. Which TWO steps are reasonable troubleshooting actions? (Select all that apply)
options: Test with a different user account
answer: Test with a different user account
questions.explanation: Testing another account helps isolate profile corruption or user-specific settings.

questions.question: Which TWO package managers are commonly associated with Linux distributions? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: apt is common on Debian-based distributions, and dnf is common on Fedora and related distributions.

questions.question: Which TWO macOS components are related to application security controls? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Gatekeeper enforces app launch policies, and notarization helps verify software checked by Apple.

questions.question: Which TWO commands can display network path or route information? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: tracert on Windows and traceroute on Linux or macOS show hops along a network path.

questions.question: Which TWO Windows features can help protect credentials or access to sensitive data? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Credential Manager stores saved credentials, and BitLocker encrypts volumes containing sensitive data.

questions.question: A computer is slow immediately after sign-in. Which TWO areas should be reviewed first? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Excess startup apps and high-resource background processes commonly cause poor performance after sign-in.

questions.question: The default modern Windows file system is ___.
questions.explanation: NTFS is the standard Windows file system and supports permissions, encryption, compression, and journaling.

questions.question: In Windows, the ___ command displays IP address, subnet mask, gateway, and DNS information.
questions.explanation: The ipconfig command displays Windows network adapter configuration.

questions.question: The Linux command that prints the current working directory is ___.
questions.explanation: pwd stands for print working directory and displays the current directory path.

questions.question: The macOS backup feature named ___ creates versioned backups of files and system data.
questions.explanation: Time Machine is the built-in macOS backup solution.

questions.question: On Linux, the ___ command changes file or directory permission bits.
questions.explanation: chmod modifies read, write, and execute permissions.

questions.question: In Linux, the ___ account has the highest administrative privileges.
questions.explanation: The root account is the superuser account with broad administrative authority.

questions.question: On many modern Linux distributions, ___ is used to manage services and systemd units.
questions.explanation: systemctl is the primary command for controlling services on systemd-based systems.

questions.question: Windows full-volume encryption is provided by ___.
questions.explanation: BitLocker encrypts Windows volumes to help protect data if hardware is lost or stolen.

questions.question: The Windows feature that rolls back system files and settings to a restore point is ___.
questions.explanation: System Restore uses restore points to reverse certain system changes.

questions.question: The Windows command-line partitioning utility is ___.
questions.explanation: diskpart is used to manage disks, partitions, and volumes from the command line.

questions.question: An automatically assigned Windows IPv4 address beginning with 169.254 is called an ___ address.
questions.explanation: APIPA addresses are self-assigned when DHCP is unavailable.

questions.question: The process of returning a mobile device to its original software state is commonly called a ___.
questions.explanation: A factory reset erases user data and restores default device settings.

questions.question: The Windows tool used to edit local or domain policy settings is Group Policy ___.
questions.explanation: Group Policy Editor is used to configure policy settings where supported.

questions.question: On Debian-based Linux systems, ___ refreshes package metadata before upgrades or installs.
questions.explanation: apt update downloads current package index information from configured repositories.

questions.question: The macOS command-line service management tool is ___.
questions.explanation: launchctl manages launchd services and agents on macOS.

questions.question: In Windows, the ___ command lists running processes from the command line.
questions.explanation: tasklist displays currently running processes and their process IDs.

questions.question: An installation that replaces the existing OS and generally requires reinstalling applications is a ___ install.
questions.explanation: A clean install starts with a fresh OS environment rather than preserving the old application setup.

questions.question: On many Linux systems, the common bootloader is ___.
questions.explanation: GRUB is a widely used bootloader for Linux systems.

questions.question: A file that stores a virtual machine's emulated hard drive is commonly called a virtual ___.
questions.explanation: A virtual disk file provides storage for the guest operating system and its data.

questions.question: The Windows startup mode that loads minimal drivers and services for troubleshooting is ___.
questions.explanation: Safe Mode reduces loaded components so technicians can isolate drivers, services, and startup issues.

questions.question: Match each Windows tool to its primary purpose.
questions.explanation: Each tool handles a specific administrative function in Windows.

questions.question: Match each Linux directory to its common purpose.
questions.explanation: Linux uses standard directories to organize configuration, user data, logs, and temporary data.

questions.question: Match each command to its function.
targets: Show network path hops in Windows

answer.tracert: Show network path hops in Windows

questions.explanation: These network commands help verify connectivity, routing paths, local addressing, and DNS resolution.

questions.question: Match each macOS feature or tool to its purpose.
targets: Store and manage passwords and certificates

answer.Keychain Access: Store and manage passwords and certificates

questions.explanation: These macOS tools support security, backup, storage repair, and credential management.

questions.question: Match each permission concept to its meaning.
targets: Run a file or traverse a directory
Perform all standard actions and manage permissions

answer.Execute: Run a file or traverse a directory
answer.Full control: Perform all standard actions and manage permissions

questions.explanation: Permissions define which actions users or groups can perform on files and directories.

questions.question: Match each update-related term to its description.
targets: Returns to a previous version or state

answer.Rollback: Returns to a previous version or state

questions.explanation: Different update types address features, security, hardware compatibility, or recovery from problems.

questions.question: Match each storage term to its description.
targets: A defined section of a physical disk
A formatted storage area accessible to the OS
A Windows identifier such as C:
A directory path used to access a file system

answer.Partition: A defined section of a physical disk
answer.Volume: A formatted storage area accessible to the OS
answer.Drive letter: A Windows identifier such as C:
answer.Mount point: A directory path used to access a file system

questions.explanation: Operating systems expose storage through partitions, volumes, drive letters, and mount points.

questions.question: Match each recovery option to its likely use.
targets: Revert Windows system settings to a restore point
Reinstall Windows with optional file preservation
Restore macOS files or system data from backup

answer.System Restore: Revert Windows system settings to a restore point
answer.Reset this PC: Reinstall Windows with optional file preservation
answer.Time Machine restore: Restore macOS files or system data from backup

questions.explanation: Recovery choices vary depending on whether the problem is boot-related, configuration-related, or requires reinstall or backup restore.

questions.question: Match each mobile OS security feature to its purpose.
targets: Erase data from a missing device

answer.Remote wipe: Erase data from a missing device

questions.explanation: Mobile security combines authentication, remote management, and permission controls.

questions.question: Match each virtualization term to its meaning.
targets: Operating system running on physical hardware
Operating system running inside a virtual machine
Saved VM state used for rollback
Software or firmware that runs virtual machines

answer.Host OS: Operating system running on physical hardware
answer.Guest OS: Operating system running inside a virtual machine
answer.Snapshot: Saved VM state used for rollback
answer.Hypervisor: Software or firmware that runs virtual machines

questions.explanation: Virtualization separates the physical host, guest systems, rollback states, and the hypervisor layer.

questions.question: Match each network setting to its purpose.
targets: Identifies the host on a network

answer.IP address: Identifies the host on a network

questions.explanation: These settings allow a device to communicate locally, route externally, and resolve names.

questions.question: Match each symptom to a likely first diagnostic area.
items: Application crashes for one user only
questions.explanation: Matching symptoms to likely causes helps narrow troubleshooting efficiently.

questions.question: Arrange the basic operating system boot process in the correct order.
questions.explanation: Firmware runs first, then the bootloader starts the kernel, services initialize, and the logon screen becomes available.

questions.question: Arrange a typical clean operating system installation workflow in order.
items: Create user account and initial settings
answer: Create user account and initial settings
questions.explanation: Backups should happen before installation, then the technician boots media, selects storage, completes setup, and patches the system.

questions.question: Arrange a safe major OS update process in order.
questions.explanation: Planning and backups should come before installation, followed by restart and validation.

questions.question: Arrange the general troubleshooting methodology in order.
items: Establish a theory of probable cause
answer: Establish a theory of probable cause
questions.explanation: Troubleshooting begins by identifying the problem, then testing causes, verifying the fix, and documenting the outcome.

questions.question: Arrange a practical Windows boot recovery escalation path in order.
items: Confirm firmware detects the system drive
Attempt System Restore if restore points exist
Back up data from recovery media if needed
Reset or reinstall Windows as a last resort
answer: Confirm firmware detects the system drive
Attempt System Restore if restore points exist
Back up data from recovery media if needed
Reset or reinstall Windows as a last resort
questions.explanation: Recovery should start with basic detection and less destructive repairs before backup, reset, or reinstall.

questions.question: Arrange the steps to create a secure new user account in order.
items: Verify the user can access required resources
Set an initial password or sign-in method
Document the account request or approval
answer: Document the account request or approval
Set an initial password or sign-in method
Verify the user can access required resources
questions.explanation: Account creation should be authorized, secured, assigned least-privilege access, and verified.

questions.question: Arrange the steps to prepare a new internal disk for use in Windows.
items: Format the volume with a file system
Assign a drive letter or mount point
answer: Format the volume with a file system
Assign a drive letter or mount point
questions.explanation: A new disk must be opened in a management tool, initialized, partitioned as a volume, formatted, and made accessible.

questions.question: Arrange a workstation hardening workflow in a reasonable order.
questions.explanation: Hardening commonly starts with patching, protection, security controls, least privilege, and validation that users can still work.
